author: garyrong
content:
Can you elaborate it? 

Currently (before Amsterdam), we charge the CALL intrinsic cost (callee access cost, memory, transfer value, potential account-creation, delegate access), then forward 63/64 to child frame.

If child frame is failed due to (1) depth check (2) transfer balance check, the child frame is reverted without affecting the parent.

If we move the sanity checks into the parent frame, when processing the CALL opcode itself, will we halt the parent? or we silently ignore the error, aborting the child frame and continue? The latter one feels strange

author: garyrong
content: And a general question, what's the main motivation to pair the account warming and bal inclusion? In 2929 accessList, we warm the accounts without accessing them.

author: draganrakita
content: This is about implementation, on protocol level you pay 100gas always, and depending on stack or state values additional gas is added.

author: draganrakita
content: I agree with this, i looks like 3th option would make it harded to implement, so we should default to 1th.

author: garyrong
content:
OK, so it's (2), no halt. 

IIUC, with the current spec, if sanity check is failed, we only warm the delegate without accessing it? https://github.com/ethereum/EIPs/blob/master/EIPS/eip-7928.md#eip-7702-delegation

If we don't need to pair the warming and bal inclusion strictly, relocating the sanity check is probably not worthwhile as it only affects the warming and cold-access charging, the delegate will not show up in the BAL in the first place.

author: draganrakita
content: Halting would be big breaking change. If we want most optimal solution than we should do 3th proposal, i dont thing anyone was considering 2th proposal. As these are small optimizations going,  1th proposal is okay and as i read it most of clients want it.

author: yperbasis
content: Sorry about late reply ( <@954738828445372416> has been recently working on Glamsterdam on our side, but he's away a.t.m.) Option 3 might be a slight win semantically, but it's not that trivial to implement in <@&688101493978562687> , so our preference in Option 1.

author: chfast
content: For evmone the depth and balance check happens on the caller side, so this is an implementation detail. And we don't fail the caller, however it is cheaper to handle this before creating a new call frame.

author: spencer_tb
content: Is everyone happy if we go with 1 for the first EELS glamsterdam devnet 6 release?
